mechanism_narrative: Persistent low inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, indicates a benign economic environment allowing for lower discount rates and higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL consistently remains low (YoY growth < 2%) but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are predominantly negative or flat (average return < 0.1%) over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above a critical threshold signals a deteriorating labor market, which typically precedes or coincides with economic contractions and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with robust payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) indicates healthy consumer demand and a strong labor market, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and PAYEMS show sustained growth above their respective thresholds for 3 consecutive months, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative or flat (average return < 0.05%) over a 1-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, rising FEDFUNDS rates increase the cost of capital and reduce the present value of future earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS consistently rises by more than 0.25 percentage points in a month during a declared tightening cycle, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are predominantly positive (average return > 0.5%) over a 1-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts (HOUST) reflects strong underlying economic demand and consumer confidence, which typically translates into broader economic expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:housing is a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If HOUST shows consistent month-over-month growth for 6 consecutive months, but S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative on average during this period (average return < 0%).

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below a critical level indicates weakening manufacturing activity and broader economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO's 3-month average growth falls below -0.5%, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such triggered instances over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high and the Federal Reserve (FEDFUNDS) is actively tightening, it signals a restrictive monetary policy aimed at cooling an overheating economy, which typically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 5% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Fed fights high inflation with rate hikes
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 5% and FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.5% over 3 months, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive or flat (average return < 0.1%) over a 1-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong labor market expansion, indicated by consistently low unemployment (UNRATE) and robust payroll growth (PAYEMS), consumer spending and corporate profits are supported, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If UNRATE remains below 5% and PAYEMS shows 3-month growth above 0.2% for 6 consecutive months, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative on average during this period (average return < 0%).
